Iku-Turso Review: 5-Star Nature Hero Guide (Stats, Emblems & Strategy)

He might look like a Scooby-Doo villain, but Iku-Turso's ability to steal healing and drown enemies in water damage makes him a unique tactical threat. We analyze if this swampy sniper still has a place in your roster against the modern titans of power creep.
Iku-Turso: The Deep-Sea Doctor Doom of Healing
Meet Iku-Turso, the "Beast of the Depths" and arguably the face only a mother (or a very desperate summoner) could love. Hailing from the

event, this Nature (Green) 5-star Legendary hero was released back in the summer of 2022. While he might look like a reject from a low-budget monster movie, his kit brings a very specific set of skills designed to make enemy healers regret their life choices.

speed sniper-hybrid, hitting three targets and applying a nasty Water damage-over-time (DoT) effect. But his real party trick is stealing healing, turning your opponent's life-saving spells into his own personal health potion.

Let's be honest: Iku-Turso is wonderfully grotesque. He looks like a cross between a frog, a fish, and a bad nightmare, complete with bulging eyes and a mouth full of needle-like teeth. The green and swampy aesthetic fits his nature element perfectly, though he definitely leans more into the "wild monster" vibe than the "noble forest guardian." He is affectionately known by the community as "The Frog," "Fish-Frog," or simply "The Beast." It's a design that stands out, even if you wouldn't invite him to a dinner party.
As a 2022 hero, Iku-Turso's base stats (811 Attack / 791 Defense / 1474 Health) have fallen victim to significant power creep.
His 811 attack was respectable in his prime but is now considered average to low compared to modern attackers who often push 1000+ base attack.
Sitting at roughly 790 Defense and 1470 Health, he is quite squishy by 2026 standards. A modern sniper can often one-shot him without defensive buffs.
To make him survivable in today's Diamond Arena or top-tier Wars, he absolutely requires
and full emblems. Without them, he is simply too fragile to withstand the barrage of modern special skills.
"Dreaded Waters" is a multi-faceted skill that offers damage, utility, and sustainability.
speed is a solid foundation. While not a team-wiper on its own, it softens up the enemy lines significantly.
The water damage is a nice bonus, chipping away health over 3 turns. The key here is the anti-Fire component: against Red heroes (who you'd typically bring against him), the DoT becomes
and lasts longer. This forces the attacker to bring a cleanser that
Fire or just eat the damage.
This is the crown jewel. Stealing 51% of
healing applied to the targets is massive. If an enemy healer fires while this debuff is active, they heal Iku-Turso instead of their allies. Like the water damage, this is also
against Fire heroes, making him a nightmare for mono-Red teams relying on healers like Garnet or Hathor (if they don't block ailments first).
Fast. He charges in 8 tiles (7 with a high-level mana troop or bard/family bonus).
Direct Damage: 280% x 3 targets = 840% total damage ratio.
DoT Damage: 243 over 3 turns (81 per turn). If hitting 3 targets, that's an additional 729 damage total.
~1569% combined damage ratio over 3 turns (assuming all targets survive).

If an enemy healer attempts to heal a target for 1000 HP, Iku-Turso steals 510 of that HP. The enemy only receives 490 HP. This effectively halves the enemy's sustain while boosting his own survivability.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Kalevala family brings strong defensive perks that are crucial for Iku-Turso's survival.

Reducing damage from the stronger element (Fire) is fantastic for a tank or flank. This makes him surprisingly durable against red stacks, forcing enemies to bring neutral colors or rely on tile damage.

is a clutch passive. Getting a 15% heal and 15% mana boost when dropping to low health can turn the tide of a match. It often allows him to fire one last desperate "Dreaded Waters" before dying, potentially stealing enough health to stay alive even longer.

is a simple but effective boost. Since his special skill damage and DoT scaling rely on his attack stat, this helps him keep up with modern damage dealers, though a defensive power might have helped his survivability more.
Upon release, Iku-Turso was considered a strong, if not overpowered, option. His fast speed and punish-the-counter mechanics (uncleansable vs Red) made him a very annoying flank in wars.
The game has moved on. Heroes now regularly hit all enemies for 400%+ or have stats that dwarf Iku-Turso's. His direct damage (280%) feels soft against modern defenses with 2500+ HP.
Iku-Turso has transitioned from a primary threat to a
. He is no longer a "must-level" for general defense. However, he remains

He is excellent for attacking teams that rely heavily on healing (e.g., facing double healer defenses).
His specific hate for Fire heroes makes him a decent choice if you know you are facing a heavy Red defense or need to bait Red hits.
Iku-Turso needs to hit hard to make his DoT sting, but he also needs to survive to steal that healing.

path is recommended. If you are bringing a sniper/hybrid, you need them to threaten a kill. Relying on him purely for survival and utility is risky in the current high-damage meta.
Unique utility that cripples enemy sustain and heals self.
Uncleansable ailments against his counter-element make him very sticky against Red teams.
Charges quickly to disrupt enemy tempo.

Family bonus reduces damage from Fire specials.

He provides ailments, but no buffs to his team (other than self-heal).

Nature has many incredible fast hitters (e.g., classic heroes with costumes, new Construct/Goblin heroes) that compete for his spot.
Great for picking apart teams that rely on a tanky healer center or heavy sustain.
A solid option for your 3rd or 4th war team, especially against Red-heavy defenses or to counter a pesky healer.

Evelyn strips the armor, allowing Iku-Turso to hit significantly harder. The follow-up from another fast sniper ensures a kill.

He is fast, but Very Fast heroes can take him out before he fires.

If you pulled Iku-Turso early, celebrate! He is a fantastic upgrade over any Classic 4-star or 5-star hero. He will carry you through map stages and lower-tier raids easily. Prioritize leveling him as your main Green damage dealer until you find modern meta heroes.

He is likely a "depth" hero for you. Unless you are severely lacking in Green attackers, he probably won't make your primary defense team. He is worth ascending if you have surplus Tonics, but think twice before committing Alpha Aethers (LB2) unless you really love his playstyle or artwork. There are stronger Green options in the current portal rotations.
The community reaction to Iku-Turso was mixed but generally positive about his utility.
Many players commented on his looks, with one forum user saying,
"In my opinion this is the green hero with the ugliest face. He looks like a monster in Scooby Doo movie."
, but most agreed Iku-Turso was the superior version due to the secondary effects. As one user put it:
"He hits hard, puts DoT on you, and on top you can’t fully heal... He’s better than a lot of naysayers are giving him credit for."
Some players successfully used him as a tank early on because of his resistance to Fire specials and uncleansable ailments against Red attackers.
"He is probably strongest green tank in game
... Forces you to go non-strong element versus him."

Iku-Turso is a "fair" hero in an unfair era. He does exactly what he says on the tin: hits three people, makes them wet, and steals their medicine. While he lacks the sheer destructive power of modern "god-tier" heroes, his utility is timeless. If you need to shut down a healer and punish a Red stack, the Frog is still your friend. Just don't expect him to win a beauty contest anytime soon.
Solid for War Depth and specific counter-play, but show caution with premium resources.
